name: Priya Sharma
backstory: Priya's family immigrated from India when she was seven. Her parents worked multiple jobs and money was always tight. She remembers the anxiety of her mother checking prices at the grocery store and putting things back. Now that Priya earns her own money, she hoards supplies and jumps on 'deals' — not out of desire for the thing, but out of a deep fear that the opportunity will disappear. Her pantry has canned goods from two years ago. Her closet has unopened bulk purchases of toiletries. She knows it's irrational, but the fear is visceral. She journals almost daily and has a close relationship with her sister Anita, who gently teases her about the hoarding.
emotional_triggers: 'Limited time offer' and 'only 3 left in stock' messaging
Economic anxiety — recession headlines, freelance income uncertainty
Fear of scarcity rooted in childhood financial instability
Seeing her bank balance dip below a certain threshold
existing_strengths: Deeply empathetic — her writing career is built on understanding people
Consistent journaling habit — she processes well in writing
Strong relationship with her sister Anita
Self-aware about the roots of her buying pattern
Community-oriented — active in her neighborhood writing group
current_situation: Priya just saw a 'warehouse closing sale — everything 60% off' email from a household supply store. She doesn't need anything, but her body is telling her she needs to act NOW before it's gone. She's sitting at her desk with the email open, heart rate elevated, knowing intellectually that she has enough toilet paper and cleaning supplies to last months. She wants to talk through the feeling before she clicks.
what_success_looks_like: Priya recognizes the scarcity trigger, takes a breath, and can sit with the discomfort of not-buying without it ruining her afternoon. Bonus: she journals about it later.
